name: pending-stub
description: Fix execute ceremony AC self-check gap (CEREMONY-012); harden chaos-mesh and wiremock charts to HA standard (TEST-004a, TEST-005a) to unblock the TEST-006/TEST-011/TEST-012 chain; create kind smoke test scaffold (HA-005a); clear HA-008 and RESTRUCTURE-001b-2 review confirmations.
sprintGoal: Harden E13 testing charts to HA standard and fix the execute ceremony self-check gap — unblocking TEST-006 and the Chaos Mesh CRD chain while building the kind smoke test scaffold.

stories.returnedReason: Execute created chart templates but proof found helm lint or AC grep commands failed; chart needs correct PDB/podAntiAffinity template structure verified against exact AC commands before marking done
stories.title: Enhance platform/charts/chaos-mesh/: add PDB, podAntiAffinity, probes, and resource limits
stories.description: Update the existing platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ Helm chart to meet the sovereign HA standard. Add a PodDisruptionBudget template with minAvailable=1, podAntiAffinity on hostname for chaos-controller-manager, readinessProbe and livenessProbe on chaos-controller-manager, and resource requests/limits on all containers. Set replicaCount>=2 for chaos-controller-manager in values.yaml. This is a static-verifiable change — no running cluster required.
acceptanceCriteria: helm lint platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ exits 0 with '0 chart(s) failed'
helm template platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ | grep PodDisruptionBudget returns at least 1 match
helm template platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ | grep podAntiAffinity returns at least 1 match
grep replicaCount platform/charts/chaos-mesh/values.yaml shows a value >= 2
stories.testPlan: helm lint platform/charts/chaos-mesh/; helm template platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ | grep PodDisruptionBudget; helm template platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ | grep podAntiAffinity; grep replicaCount platform/charts/chaos-mesh/values.yaml; show all output verbatim.

stories.returnedReason: Execute created wiremock chart templates but proof found AC grep commands failed; chart needs correct PDB/podAntiAffinity template structure verified against exact AC commands
stories.title: Enhance platform/charts/wiremock/: add PDB, podAntiAffinity, probes, and resource limits
stories.description: Update the existing platform/charts/wiremock/ Helm chart to meet the sovereign HA standard. Add a PodDisruptionBudget template with minAvailable=1, podAntiAffinity on hostname, readinessProbe and livenessProbe, and resource requests/limits on all containers. Set replicaCount>=2 in values.yaml. This is a static-verifiable change — no running cluster required.
acceptanceCriteria: helm lint platform/charts/wiremock/ exits 0 with '0 chart(s) failed'
helm template platform/charts/wiremock/ | grep PodDisruptionBudget returns at least 1 match
helm template platform/charts/wiremock/ | grep podAntiAffinity returns at least 1 match

stories.testPlan: helm lint platform/charts/wiremock/; helm template platform/charts/wiremock/ | grep PodDisruptionBudget; helm template platform/charts/wiremock/ | grep podAntiAffinity; grep replicaCount platform/charts/wiremock/values.yaml; show all output verbatim.

stories.returnedReason: Execute created a shell script artifact but proof found shellcheck errors; script must be authored with shellcheck passing before marking done
stories.title: Create scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh: static-verifiable scaffold covering PLATFORM-001 through PLATFORM-004
stories.description: Create scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh that encodes all PLATFORM-001 through PLATFORM-004 acceptance criteria as executable test functions (cert-manager, sealed-secrets, Harbor, Keycloak). The script must define one test function per PLATFORM story, print a PASS/FAIL summary table, and pass shellcheck -S error with no warnings. The exact commands for each test come from the acceptance criteria in prd/increment-21-platform-foundations.json.
acceptanceCriteria: shellcheck -S error scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh exits 0
grep -c 'helm\|kubectl' scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh returns a value >= 4 (at least one invocation per PLATFORM-00x test)
grep -E 'PASS|FAIL' scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh exits 0 (PASS and FAIL literals appear in summary output logic)
stories.testPlan: shellcheck -S error scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh; grep -c 'helm\|kubectl' scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh; grep -E 'PASS|FAIL' scripts/test/kind-smoke.sh; show all output verbatim.

stories.title: Chaos Mesh PodChaos experiment YAML + README validates PDB prevents simultaneous pod eviction
stories.description: Create test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml: a Chaos Mesh PodChaos experiment targeting a platform service (e.g. grafana namespace, mode: all) to simultaneously terminate all pods. The PDB minAvailable:1 should prevent the last pod from being evicted. Create test/chaos/README.md explaining how to apply the experiment against a kind cluster running Chaos Mesh (TEST-004), what PDB-enforcement behavior to observe, and how to clean up. The YAML is a static test artifact — it does not run in CI. This covers the 'PDB chaos validation' goal from E15.
acceptanceCriteria: test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml exists — ls test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml exits 0
python3 -c "import yaml, sys; yaml.safe_load(open(sys.argv[1]).read())" test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml exits 0 (valid YAML)
grep 'kind: PodChaos' test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml exits 0
grep 'mode: all' test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml exits 0 (targets all replicas to exercise PDB)
test/chaos/README.md exists and grep -i 'PodDisruptionBudget\|PDB' test/chaos/README.md exits 0
stories.testPlan: ls test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml test/chaos/README.md && python3 -c "import yaml, sys; yaml.safe_load(open(sys.argv[1]).read())" test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml && grep 'kind: PodChaos' test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml && grep 'mode: all' test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml && grep -i 'PDB' test/chaos/README.md; show all output verbatim

stories.title: platform/deploy.sh: Helm deployment orchestrator with --help, --chart-dir, --namespace, and helm upgrade --install invocation
stories.description: Create platform/deploy.sh. The script must: (1) support --help flag that prints usage including '--chart-dir DIR' and '--namespace NS'; (2) accept --chart-dir DIR and --namespace NS required options; (3) print 'Deploying chart: $chart_dir to namespace: $namespace' before running; (4) invoke 'helm upgrade --install' for the given chart and namespace. Script must pass shellcheck -S error with set -euo pipefail at the top.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/deploy.sh exists: ls platform/deploy.sh exits 0
shellcheck -S error platform/deploy.sh exits 0
bash -n platform/deploy.sh exits 0
bash platform/deploy.sh --help 2>&1 | grep -q 'chart-dir' exits 0
grep -q 'helm upgrade --install' platform/deploy.sh exits 0 (script contains the helm invocation)
stories.testPlan: ls platform/deploy.sh && shellcheck -S error platform/deploy.sh && bash -n platform/deploy.sh && bash platform/deploy.sh --help 2>&1 | grep 'chart-dir' && grep 'helm upgrade --install' platform/deploy.sh && echo 'all checks passed'; show all output verbatim

smart.notes: Specific score 4: exact file named and behavior documented, but error handling for missing required args (--chart-dir, --namespace) is unspecified. Measurable score 4: AC verifies the string 'helm upgrade --install' exists in the file but does not verify --chart-dir/--namespace are actually wired through to the helm invocation.

stories.title: Enforce AC self-check in execute ceremony: require output of all AC commands before marking passes:true
stories.description: In sprint 33, five stories (HA-005a, TEST-004a, TEST-005a, TEST-006, KAIZEN-015) all reached [PROOF-FAIL]: the execute phase created plausible artifacts but did not run the story's AC commands before marking done. The execute ceremony must explicitly require that all AC commands are run and their output shown verbatim before passes:true is set. Add a mandatory 'self-check' step to the execute ceremony prompt: after creating artifacts, run every AC command from the story's acceptanceCriteria array and show the full output. If any AC command fails, keep passes:false and report the failure.
acceptanceCriteria: grep -n 'self.check\|acceptanceCriteria\|run.*AC\|verify.*criteria' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/execute.md | wc -l returns a value >= 3 (at least 3 lines referencing AC verification in the execute ceremony)
python3 -m py_compile scripts/ralph/ceremonies.py exits 0
shellcheck -S error scripts/ralph/ceremonies.sh exits 0
stories.testPlan: grep -n 'self.check\|acceptanceCriteria\|run.*AC\|verify.*criteria' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/execute.md | wc -l; python3 -m py_compile scripts/ralph/ceremonies.py; shellcheck -S error scripts/ralph/ceremonies.sh; show all output verbatim.
stories.returnedReason: Remediation story: execute-without-self-verification caused 5 stories to reach proof-fail; root cause is execute ceremony does not mandate running AC commands before marking passes:true

smart.notes: Specific score 4: execute ceremony currently runs via ralph-prompt.md (not execute.md); description says 'add to execute ceremony prompt' but AC targets scripts/ralph/ceremonies/execute.md which does not yet exist — how execute.md gets wired into ceremonies.py is unspecified. Measurable score 4: AC1 grep count (>=3 lines) is a proxy for self-check language presence, not a behavioral test that the ceremony actually runs AC commands.
